paper_title: Analysis of Pollen Concentrations and Airborne Pollutants in the Madrid Region

methods.description: This method involves collecting daily data on pollen grain concentrations and airborne pollutant concentrations, followed by calculating monthly means for each measuring station and year.

steps.description: The information provided on the variables for 'pollen grain concentrations' and 'airborne pollutant concentrations' refers to daily data.
steps.searchable_summary: collect daily data on pollen and pollutants

steps.description: These were used to calculate the monthly means for each measuring station and for each year in the study.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate monthly means from daily data

methods.description: This method uses statistical tools to calculate the degree of correlation between pollen concentrations and airborne pollutants, employing Spearman's rank correlation coefficient.

steps.description: The data analysis tool, Statgraphics Centurion 18, was applied to calculate the degree of correlation between both variables.
steps.searchable_summary: use Statgraphics Centurion 18 to calculate correlation

steps.description: A multivariable analysis was conducted, calculating Spearman's linear correlation coefficient for the pollen types with each of the six variables for the six airborne pollutants in the study.
The correlation was first calculated for all the data from all the pollen concentrations and air pollutant measuring stations, and for the total years in the study.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate overall correlation for all data

steps.description: The correlation was then calculated for each of the 11 study zones.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation for each study zone

methods.method_name: Analysis of Variance and Multiple Rank Test
methods.description: This method involves performing an analysis of variance to determine significant differences in mean values, followed by a multiple rank test to identify specific year levels with significant differences.

steps.description: An analysis of variance was carried out on the two types of variables to determine whether there is any significant difference in their mean values between one year level and another.
steps.searchable_summary: perform analysis of variance on variables

steps.description: In cases where the analysis of variance concluded that there is a significant difference, a multiple rank test was carried out using Fisher's least significant difference (LSD) method.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct multiple rank test using Fisher's LSD
